title: teucrium chamaedrys subsp. gracile
intro: Teucrium chamaedrys subsp. gracile, also known as the small-leaved wood mint or small-leaved germander, is a subspecies of the Teucrium chamaedrys plant, a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Teucrium chamaedrys subsp. gracile include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as its potential to act as an antimicrobial and antifungal agent. Teucrium chamaedrys subsp. gracile can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
